user_query:
I'm a family doctor who writes short briefs for nearby clinics. I have a rough script a colleague gave me for pulling general info from the World Health Organization website, but it’s brittle and not clinic-ready. Please do a focused code review and refactor to make it a small, dependable pipeline I can run locally without extra packages.

Inputs you must use:
- input/fetcher.py: the existing one-off script to review and refactor.
- input/config.yaml: configuration with the official domain (who.int), path (/), and required output paths.

What to build (using only the Python standard library):
1) Refactor the logic in input/fetcher.py into a small, testable module under app/ (e.g., app/net.py and app/extract.py) with clear functions and docstrings. Eliminate hardcoded paths and read settings from input/config.yaml. Keep responsibilities separated: network fetch, disk I/O, and HTML-to-text extraction should be distinct.
2) Implement a runnable entry point at scripts/run_pipeline.py that:
   - Reads input/config.yaml.
   - Downloads the HTML of the official World Health Organization homepage identified by the domain in the config (who.int) and the configured path.
   - Saves raw HTML to the configured raw_html_path (data/raw/page.html), creating directories as needed.
   - Extracts plain text from the HTML (strip tags, normalize whitespace) and saves it to the configured text_path (data/processed/page.txt).
   - Writes a JSON metadata file to the configured metadata_path (data/processed/metadata.json) with at least: {"url", "fetched_at" (ISO 8601), "title" (from <title> if present, else empty string), "word_count", "html_bytes", "text_chars"}.
3) Add a validation script at scripts/validate.py that performs sanity checks and returns a non-zero exit code on failure. Minimum checks:
   - All three artifacts exist at the configured paths.
   - Raw HTML is non-empty and contains the string "<html".
   - Extracted text is non-empty and contains at least 50 words.
   - metadata.json parses as JSON and includes all required keys listed above.
   The script should print a concise pass/fail summary to stdout.
4) Run the validation script and save its console output to reports/validation_summary.txt. The validation command must be part of your deliverable and runnable locally.
5) Compose a short status report at reports/refactor_status.md with three sections:
   - "Refactoring changes" (3–7 bullet points describing the main improvements).
   - "How to run" (the exact commands to run the pipeline and validation locally).
   - "Clinic relevance" (2–3 sentences on how a reliable fetch-and-extract step helps small clinics stay informed).

Key constraints:
- Use only the Python standard library (no third-party packages).
- Do not change the domain in the config; the homepage must be fetched from the official WHO site identified by who.int and the path in input/config.yaml.
- Keep paths relative to the workspace and ensure the exact output files are created at:
  - data/raw/page.html
  - data/processed/page.txt
  - data/processed/metadata.json
  - reports/validation_summary.txt
  - reports/refactor_status.md

Deliverables should include the refactored code under app/, the runnable scripts under scripts/, the generated artifacts under data/ and reports/, and the updated code should pass your validation script. Please make sure the Internet download and HTML extraction actually occur as part of the pipeline, the validation command can be run locally, and the status report clearly summarizes the changes and relevance for clinics. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
